comments.author: codex
comments.text: Implemented in index.ts: computeSlack() backward pass (CPM latest-start/finish from project end + downstream deadlines); slack_days CSV column + tasks[].slack_days/infeasible JSON; infeasibleWarnings() emitted on stderr by command + exporter; ▼TODAY marker line in renderGantt; computeSummary() + HTML Summary footer + Assignee workload table. Added 11 unit tests (23 total, all green). Functionally verified in /tmp/gantttest with a 6-item dependency chain and tight deadlines: slack column correct (off-path Write docs = +1, critical = 0/-7), infeasibility warnings fired, TODAY marker rendered, HTML summary + workload present. Backward-compatible: slack blank without --schedule, mermaid/html/ascii unchanged otherwise.

notes.author: codex
notes.text: Verified end-to-end in throwaway pm workspace: build clean, tsc --noEmit clean, 2/2 tests pass. extension doctor: active 1, runtime_active 1, 0 failures. pm gantt (default) backward-compatible; --group-by assignee, --critical-path (marks A->B->C->E 4-item chain, excludes shorter A->D branch), --from all work. pm gantt export --format mermaid|html|ascii verified well-formed. Bad --format/--from exit 2 (USAGE), no double-invocation. SDK: registerCommand with flags REQUIRES 'schema' capability in manifest or activation throws; kept schema, dropped unused renderers.

notes.author: codex
notes.text: FAIL-vs-WARN policy: HARD-FAIL only on a dependency CYCLE — a cycle has no valid topological order, so forward scheduling is impossible and the existing render-path cycle guards would silently break the cycle and emit a plausible-but-wrong chart (the confusing-data case this gate stops). WARN (non-blocking, stderr) on soft issues that still yield a useful chart: deadline-before-its-own-start, and an implausibly-large estimate (>2000h, likely minutes-vs-hours error). Deliberately did NOT hard-fail or duplicate the infeasible/unreachable-deadline check — round-3 already FLAGS that in the render via infeasibleWarnings; chart still useful so it stays a warning. Gate kept judicious so normal charts never block.

notes.author: codex
notes.text: SDK swallow-throw finding (verified functionally): pm runtime wraps registerPreflight overrides in try/catch and downgrades a thrown error to a non-fatal warning, so a throw inside registerPreflight does NOT abort the command. Therefore the real enforcement lives in the command/exporter HANDLER via runDataSanityGate() which throws the package CommandError(EXIT_CODE.USAGE) to truly fail fast. A scoped pass-through registerPreflight is still registered (returns empty delta, no-op for non-gantt commands) so the extension truthfully advertises the 'preflight' capability. Also kept 'schema' in the manifest because registerCommand declares a flags:[] array (SDK requires 'schema' for flag declarations).

notes.author: codex
notes.text: FUNCTIONAL EVIDENCE (real pm project /tmp/scratch-gantt, 4 Tasks w/ estimates+deadlines+blocked_by chain, extension installed --project; capabilities activated: commands,importers,preflight,schema). (1) CYCLE pm-ajjf→pm-5jnq→pm-jksp→pm-lz2x→pm-ajjf: 'pm gantt --schedule' HARD-FAILED exit=2, 0 bytes stdout (no chart), stderr named the full cycle path; also fails without --schedule (exit 2). (2) Cycle removed + deadline 2026-06-01 set before created_at 2026-06-04 on pm-ajjf: 'pm gantt --schedule' exit=0, stderr 'NOTE: 1 data-sanity warning(s) (chart still rendered): pm-ajjf Design API: deadline 2026-06-01 is before its start 2026-06-04', 41 lines of chart on stdout. (3) Fully-sane data: 'pm gantt --schedule' exit=0, no stderr, clean chart. Unit tests added (test/preflight.test.ts): 9 cases for detectCycles + dataSanityReport. Full suite 31/31 pass; build + tsc --noEmit clean.
